seo.title: Building Research Agents with Google ADK + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build research agents with Google ADK + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Google's Agent Development Kit (ADK) enables developers to build sophisticated multi-agent systems powered by Gemini models, ideal for research agents that perform web search, analysis, and synthesis. However, multi-agent delegation, model switching, and long-session state management introduce risks like circular dependencies, output inconsistency, and verification failures. Vex provides essential guardrails to ensure reliable, accurate research outputs through structured verification and failure mitigation.

architectureOverview.heading: Research Agents Architecture with Google ADK
architectureOverview.description:
Research agents in Google ADK typically feature a hierarchical multi-agent structure: a Router Agent delegates tasks to specialized Search Agent, Analysis Agent, and Synthesis Agent. The Search Agent uses web tools to gather sources, passing them to the Analysis Agent for claim extraction and verification, while the Synthesis Agent compiles summaries with citations. Sessions maintain state across iterations for iterative refinement.

Gemini models power each agent, with dynamic switching for specialized tasks (e.g., Gemini 1.5 Pro for analysis). Tools include web search APIs and custom verifiers, but lack built-in safeguards against circular delegation or format mismatches between agents.

Long conversations risk state corruption, especially when agents reference outdated search results or fail recency checks, making Vex integration critical for production reliability.
architectureOverview.diagram:
Router Agent ──┐
                ├─> Search Agent (Web Tools) ──> Sources
                ├─> Analysis Agent (Gemini 1.5 Pro)
                └─> Synthesis Agent ──> Final Report
                         ↑
                    Vex Guardrails
                    (before_agent_callback)

risks.name: Multi-agent delegation creating circular dependencies
risks.description: Router endlessly delegates between agents without convergence
risks.mitigation: Vex conversation loop detection terminates cycles

risks.name: Gemini model switching causing output inconsistency
risks.description: Different Gemini variants produce incompatible formats

risks.name: Session state corruption in long conversations
risks.description: Cached search results become stale across agent handoffs
risks.mitigation: Vex recency validation and state sanitization

risks.name: Tool response format mismatches across agents
risks.description: Search tools return inconsistent JSON breaking analysis

implementation.title: Basic Research Agent with ADK
implementation.description: Core multi-agent setup with router and search delegation
implementation.code:
from google.adk.agents import RouterAgent, SearchAgent, LlmAgent
from google.adk.tools import WebSearchTool

router = RouterAgent(
    model='gemini-2.0-flash',
    tools=[WebSearchTool()]
)

search_agent = SearchAgent(
    model='gemini-1.5-pro',
    tools=[WebSearchTool()]
)

analysis_agent = LlmAgent(model='gemini-1.5-pro')

session = router.create_session(query='Latest AI research trends')
result = session.run()

implementation.title: Web Search Tool Integration
implementation.description: Configure search agent with recency filtering
implementation.code:
web_search = WebSearchTool(
    max_results=10,
    recency_days=30,
    query_template="{topic} filetype:pdf site:arxiv.org"
)

search_agent = SearchAgent(
    model='gemini-1.5-pro',
    tools=[web_search]
)

sources = search_agent.search('quantum computing breakthroughs')

implementation.title: Multi-Step Analysis Pipeline
implementation.description: Chain search → analysis → synthesis with state management
implementation.code:
def research_pipeline(query):
    # Step 1: Search
    sources = search_agent.search(query)
    
    # Step 2: Analyze claims
    claims = analysis_agent.analyze(sources)
    
    # Step 3: Synthesize
    report = synthesis_agent.summarize(claims)
    return report

result = research_pipeline('Climate change impacts 2024')

implementation.title: Session State Management
implementation.description: Maintain conversation context across agent handoffs

vexIntegration.title: Vex before_agent_callback Setup
vexIntegration.description: Add Vex as callback to catch failures before agent execution
vexIntegration.code:
from vex import VexClient
from google.adk import AgentSession

vex = VexClient(api_key='vex-your-key')

@vex.guardrail(
    verifications=[
        'source_verification',
        'claim_accuracy',
        'recency_checks',
        'bias_detection'
    ]
)
def before_agent_callback(session: AgentSession, input_data: dict) -> dict:
    """Vex validation before each agent executes"""
    verified = vex.verify(
        data=input_data,
        risk_profile='medium',
        framework='google-adk'
    )
    if not verified:
        raise ValueError('Vex guardrail failed')
    return input_data

# Register callback
router.add_callback(before_agent_callback)

vexIntegration.title: Source Verification & Recency Guardrail
vexIntegration.description: Validate search results before analysis agent
